blueprint.name: Smart Home Battery
blueprint.description: Generic Home Assistant blueprint for up to four batteries. It allocates discharge from house demand, can trigger opportunistic charging during real export, can charge during off-peak periods from a target computed from tomorrow's solar forecast, and writes a signed power target for each battery into a numeric entity, with optional custom actions for integrations that need a separate mode switch.
message.invalid_sensor: House power sensor {{ house_power_sensor }} is unavailable or not numeric.
message.small_delta_skipped: House power sensor delta below trigger threshold:
trace.stop_small_delta: Stop on small house power delta
validation.no_target_entity.suffix: is enabled by its state of charge sensor, but no Target power entity is configured. Add a `number` or `input_number` entity, or clear the state of charge sensor to disable this battery slot.
validation.no_power.suffix: has a target power entity configured, but both maximum power limits are set to 0 W. Set a non-zero Maximum discharge power or Maximum charge power to make this slot usable.
input.house_power_sensor.name: House power sensor
input.house_power_sensor.description: Main house power sensor. This selector only shows power sensors. Use a signed sensor in watts with positive values for import and negative values for export.
input.off_peak_entities.name: Active off-peak entities
input.off_peak_entities.description: Optional entities that turn on during off-peak periods. If at least one selected entity is on, the blueprint stops commanding discharge and can charge the batteries configured for off-peak charging.
input.off_peak_import_limit_w.name: Maximum house import during off-peak charging
input.off_peak_import_limit_w.descriptio: Grid import ceiling in watts during off-peak charging. The blueprint limits battery charging to keep estimated house import below this value. Set to 0 to disable off-peak charging.
input.solar_forecast_tomorrow_sensor.des: Optional energy sensor for tomorrow's solar production forecast, in kWh. A valid 0 kWh value targets 100% charge. If the sensor is empty, unavailable, or non-numeric, predictive off-peak charging does not start.
input.solar_reserve_share_pct.name: Solar forecast share to reserve
input.solar_reserve_share_pct.descriptio: Percentage of tomorrow's solar production forecast to keep free in the batteries after off-peak charging. A lower value charges more overnight; a higher value leaves more room for solar.
input.battery.soc_sensor.name: State of charge sensor
input.battery.soc_sensor.description: Leave empty to ignore this battery slot. Otherwise, this selector only shows battery sensors that report a charge percentage, and the chosen sensor enables the slot. An enabled slot must also expose a numeric target entity and at least one non-zero power limit.
input.battery.max_discharge_w.name: Maximum discharge power
input.battery.max_discharge_w.descriptio: Manual discharge limit used by the allocator for this battery.
input.battery.max_charge_w.name: Maximum charge power
input.battery.max_charge_w.description: Manual charge limit used by the allocator for opportunistic charging and off-peak charging. Charge-capable batteries can absorb residual real export once discharge targets have been trimmed down, or charge from the grid during off-peak periods when this slot allows it. Set to 0 if this battery must never be charged by the blueprint.
input.battery.usable_capacity_kwh.name: Usable capacity
input.battery.usable_capacity_kwh.descri: Usable capacity of this battery in kWh. It is only used to compute the off-peak charge target from the solar forecast.
input.battery.off_peak_charge_enabled.de: Allow this battery slot to charge during off-peak periods. The slot must also have a usable capacity, a maximum charge power, and a target entity.
input.battery.cooldown_seconds.name: Command cooldown
input.battery.cooldown_seconds.descripti: Minimum delay between two active target updates for this battery, and the main signal used to decide how reactive it is. Lower values make the battery handle residual changes first, and the shortest available battery at 10 s or less can receive small non-zero residual targets below the main command deadband. Higher values make the battery keep a steadier active target while faster batteries absorb demand changes. Set it to 0 to disable the delay entirely. Writing `0`, flipping the sign, or starting from `0` when the raw house meter confirms matching import or export still happens immediately so the blueprint can stop, reverse, or resume a useful battery without waiting. A significant raw export can also reduce an active discharge target before the cooldown expires, and smaller raw export can reduce it once that battery's cooldown has elapsed.
input.battery.target_power_entity.name: Target power entity
input.battery.target_power_entity.descri: The `number` or `input_number` entity written by the blueprint. The target is signed in watts: positive for discharge, negative for charge, `0` for stop. If this battery should charge, the selected entity must accept negative values.
input.battery.actual_power_sensor.name: Actual power sensor
input.battery.actual_power_sensor.descri: Optional power sensor that reports the battery's real output in watts. The allocator still uses the target entity and the house meter as its primary control loop. After the response grace period, a valid non-stale reading is used only as a guardrail to avoid counting a battery as real supply when it is no longer delivering. A stale reading does not prevent an idle battery from starting. If telemetry stays stale after an active target had time to react, that battery is kept out of extra discharge allocation while its already active target can stay in place unless a stop, sign change, or real export requires changing it.
input.battery.discharge_actions.name: Discharge actions
input.battery.discharge_actions.descript: Optional custom actions executed when this battery should actively discharge, and again when it should stop but still reports physical discharge. They run on every active discharge target update. Variables such as battery_slot, battery_soc, target_power_w, target_discharge_w, house_power_w, import_need_w and export_surplus_w are available inside these actions.
input.battery.charge_actions.name: Charge actions
input.battery.charge_actions.description: Optional custom actions executed when this battery should actively charge from surplus export or during off-peak periods, and again when it should stop but still reports physical charge. They run on every active charge target update. Variables such as battery_slot, battery_soc, target_power_w, target_charge_w, export_surplus_w, off_peak_active, off_peak_target_soc_pct, solar_forecast_tomorrow_kwh and solar_reserve_share_pct are available inside these actions.
